output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford received a 90-day non-interest-bearing promissory note with face value $100,000 in settlement of a trade receivable. After 50 days, Ford discounted the note at the bank at 15% annual discount rate. The note has 40 days remaining to maturity (90 - 50). Using actual/365 day-count convention, the bank discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. Management has determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9 (transfer of risks and rewards), so Ford derecognizes the note receivable and recognizes the difference between carrying amount and net proceeds as a loss. The entry records: (1) cash received from bank, (2) loss on discounting, and (3) removal of the note receivable.

output_entry_builder.reason: Ford discounted a non-interest-bearing note at the bank with derecognition. The note has a face value of $100,000 and 40 days remaining to maturity (90 - 50 = 40 days). The bank charges a discount based on 15% annual rate for 40 days. Discount = $100,000 × 0.15 × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. Since management determined this qualifies as derecognition, Ford removes the note receivable from its books and recognizes the difference between the carrying amount ($100,000) and net proceeds ($98,356.16) as a loss.
